title: Ensure that the currently missing architecture-critical language features have been implemented
analysis:
Assessed the three architecture-critical features identified in the review:

(1) Import handling — COMPLETE. Imports are parsed into AST (ImportDecl in reify-syntax), compiled into CompiledModule.imports with a warning diagnostic ('import noted; module resolution not yet implemented'), and tested via import_compiles_into_module_imports, import_produces_warning_diagnostic, and comprehensive_all_three_features tests in crates/reify-compiler/tests/boundary2_producer.rs. Module resolution is deferred to M5 by design. No implementation work needed — only Engine-level verification.

(2) Stdlib function evaluation — COMPLETE. The compiler produces CompiledExprKind::FunctionCall nodes (reify-compiler lib.rs:487-528), reify_expr::eval_expr dispatches to reify_stdlib::eval_builtin (reify-expr lib.rs:23-30), and all 26+ math functions are implemented with 41+ passing unit tests. The E2E bracket fixture uses function calls (box()), and the parse-compile pipeline handles function resolution. Functions produce correct numeric values, NOT Undef. No implementation work needed — only Engine-level verification.

(3) Sub-structure compilation — THIS IS THE PRIMARY GAP. The compiler correctly produces SubComponentDecl entries in TopologyTemplate.sub_components (verified by existing tests). However:
  - EvaluationGraph::from_templates() (graph.rs:68-115) completely ignores template.sub_components
  - Engine::eval() (lib.rs:562-938) never iterates over sub_components
  - No scoped ValueCellIds are created for child parameters
  - Child param values and let-binding values never appear in the eval result ValueMap

The implementation plan focuses on closing the sub-structure evaluation gap with TDD, plus adding Engine-level verification tests for import handling and stdlib evaluation to confirm end-to-end correctness through the Engine facade.

Key code locations:
  - Engine::eval(): crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs lines 562-938
  - EvaluationGraph::from_templates(): crates/reify-eval/src/graph.rs lines 68-115
  - TopologyTemplate + SubComponentDecl: crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs lines 28-47
  - TopologyTemplateBuilder.sub_component(): crates/reify-test-support/src/builders.rs lines 247-263
  - CompiledModuleBuilder.import(): crates/reify-test-support/src/builders.rs lines 338-344
  - extract_dependency_trace(): crates/reify-eval/src/deps.rs lines 13-17
  - topological_sort(): crates/reify-eval/src/dirty.rs (imported at lib.rs:21)
  - Existing Engine tests: crates/reify-eval/tests/boundary5_engine.rs

All 365 workspace tests currently pass. The tree-sitter grammar already supports 'sub' declarations. The syntax parser (ts_parser.rs) already lowers sub_declaration nodes into SubDecl AST. The compiler (lib.rs:782-797) already compiles SubDecl into SubComponentDecl with arg expression compilation. The only missing piece is evaluation-layer elaboration of sub-components.

prerequisites.description: Add a parent_child_module() fixture function to reify-test-support/src/fixtures.rs that returns a CompiledModule with two templates: (1) 'Child' template with param height: Scalar(LENGTH) = 10mm (0.01 SI) and let half_h: Scalar(LENGTH) = ValueRef('Child','height') / 2; (2) 'Parent' template with param width: Scalar(LENGTH) = 80mm (0.08 SI) and sub rib = Child(height: ValueRef('Parent','width') * 0.5). Uses existing TopologyTemplateBuilder (param, let_binding, sub_component methods) and CompiledModuleBuilder. The Child template must be listed first in the module so it can be found by structure_name lookup during sub-component elaboration.

steps.description: Write failing test 'sub_component_params_appear_in_eval_result' in crates/reify-eval/tests/boundary5_engine.rs. Uses parent_child_module() fixture. After Engine::eval() with MockConstraintChecker and no kernel, asserts that result.values contains ValueCellId::new('Parent.rib','height') with value approximately 0.04 (80mm * 0.5 = 40mm = 0.04 SI). This is the arg-evaluated value, not the child's default. Currently fails because Engine::eval() ignores sub_components entirely.

Implement sub-component param elaboration in Engine::eval() (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs). After the two-pass parent evaluation (after the let-binding topological sort pass, around line 741) but before the resolution phase (line 744), add a new sub-component elaboration loop. For each template, iterate template.sub_components. For each SubComponentDecl: (a) find the referenced child template in module.templates by structure_name, (b) build a child-local ValueMap seeded with the child template's original IDs, (c) for each child param, check if an arg with matching member name exists — if yes, evaluate the arg expression against the parent's values map using reify_expr::eval_expr; if no, evaluate the child param's default_expr against the child-local map, (d) insert child param values into child-local map with child's original ID e.g. ValueCellId::new('Child','height'), (e) insert into the main values map and snapshot.values with scoped ID ValueCellId::new('{template.name}.{sub.name}', param_member) and DeterminacyState::Determined. Record journal events and cache entries for each eval. Skip sub-components whose structure_name is not found (no panic).

Write failing test 'sub_component_child_lets_evaluated' in boundary5_engine.rs. Uses parent_child_module(). After Engine::eval(), asserts ValueCellId::new('Parent.rib','half_h') exists in result.values and has value approximately 0.02 (half of 0.04 = 40mm/2 = 20mm = 0.02 SI). Currently fails because child let-bindings are not evaluated during sub-component elaboration.

Extend sub-component elaboration in Engine::eval() to evaluate child let-bindings. After populating child param values in the child-local ValueMap, evaluate child let-bindings using the same topological sort pattern as the parent (extract_dependency_trace from deps.rs + topological_sort from dirty.rs). The child lets reference child-original IDs (e.g. ValueRef('Child','height')) which resolve in the child-local ValueMap. Insert results into both the child-local map and the main values map + snapshot.values with scoped IDs (e.g. ValueCellId::new('Parent.rib','half_h')) and DeterminacyState::Determined.

Write test 'sub_component_default_param_when_no_arg' in boundary5_engine.rs. Builds a module using CompiledModuleBuilder with: Child template (param height: Scalar = 10mm), Parent template (param width: Scalar = 80mm, sub rib = Child() with empty args vec). After Engine::eval(), asserts ValueCellId::new('Parent.rib','height') has the Child's default value approximately 0.01 (10mm SI). This tests the fallback-to-default-expr path when no matching arg is provided.

If step-5 fails, fix the default evaluation path in sub-component elaboration — ensure child params without matching args use their default_expr evaluated against the child-local ValueMap. If it passes (expected if step-2 correctly handles the no-arg case), this is a no-op.

Write test 'sub_component_missing_structure_skipped_gracefully' in boundary5_engine.rs. Build module using CompiledModuleBuilder with Parent template having sub_component('thing', 'NonExistent', vec![]) and no 'NonExistent' template in the module. After Engine::eval(), assert that eval completes without panicking, Parent's own param values are correctly evaluated, and result.values does not contain any 'Parent.thing' scoped entries.

Add graceful handling in Engine::eval() sub-component processing: when structure_name is not found in module.templates, push a diagnostic warning ('sub-component "{name}" references unknown structure "{structure_name}"') to the diagnostics vec and continue to the next sub-component. Should already be handled in step-2 (skip if not found), but this step verifies and adds the diagnostic message if missing.

Write test 'sub_component_nodes_in_evaluation_graph' as an inline #[cfg(test)] test in crates/reify-eval/src/graph.rs. Build EvaluationGraph::from_templates() with two templates: Child (param height, let half_h) and Parent (param width, sub rib = Child(height: width * 0.5)). Assert that graph.value_cells contains scoped entries ValueCellId::new('Parent.rib','height') and ValueCellId::new('Parent.rib','half_h'). Currently fails because from_templates() ignores sub_components.

Extend EvaluationGraph::from_templates() (crates/reify-eval/src/graph.rs lines 68-115) to process sub_components. After processing each template's value_cells/constraints/realizations, iterate template.sub_components. For each SubComponentDecl, look up the referenced template by name in the templates slice. If found, create scoped ValueCellNode entries: for each child value_cell, create a new node with id=ValueCellId::new('{parent_name}.{sub_name}', child_member), same kind/type/content_hash, and cloned default_expr. If structure_name not found, skip silently. Note: the expressions in these graph nodes still reference child-original IDs — the Engine::eval() sub-component elaboration handles the mapping at eval time via the child-local ValueMap.

Write Engine-level verification test 'engine_eval_stdlib_function_in_let' in boundary5_engine.rs. Uses parse-compile-eval pipeline (same pattern as e2e_parse_compile_eval_auto_param). Source: 'structure S { param w: Scalar = 80mm\n param h: Scalar = 100mm\n let diag = sqrt(w*w + h*h) }'. After Engine::eval() with MockConstraintChecker, assert values contain ValueCellId::new('S','diag') with value approximately 0.12806m (sqrt(0.08^2 + 0.1^2) = sqrt(0.0164) approx 0.12806). Expected to PASS immediately — stdlib functions are fully implemented. Confirms full Engine pipeline for stdlib dispatch.

If step-11 fails, debug and fix. Expected to be a no-op — stdlib evaluation is already fully working through the Engine::eval() pipeline (eval_expr dispatches to reify_stdlib::eval_builtin for FunctionCall nodes).

Write Engine-level verification test 'engine_eval_with_import' in boundary5_engine.rs. Build module via CompiledModuleBuilder::new(ModulePath::single('test')).import('std/math').template(simple_template).build(). After Engine::eval() with MockConstraintChecker, assert: (a) module.imports.len() == 1 and module.imports[0].path == 'std/math', (b) template param values are correctly evaluated (imports don't affect evaluation). Expected to PASS immediately — imports are transparent to evaluation.
If step-13 fails, debug and fix. Expected to be a no-op — imports are stored in CompiledModule.imports and the Engine ignores them during evaluation (they have no effect on the value map).

Write comprehensive E2E test 'e2e_all_three_features_through_engine' in boundary5_engine.rs. Parse and compile source combining all three features: 'import "std/math"
structure Child { param size: Scalar = 10mm
 let half = size / 2 }
structure Parent { param w: Scalar = 80mm
 let diag = sqrt(w * w)
 sub part = Child(size: w / 2)
 constraint diag > 0mm }'. After Engine::eval() then Engine::check_constraints_with_values(), verify: (a) module has 1 import, (b) values contain ('Parent','w') approx 0.08 and ('Parent','diag') approx 0.08 (sqrt of 0.08^2), (c) values contain ('Parent.part','size') approx 0.04 (80mm/2), (d) values contain ('Parent.part','half') approx 0.02 (size/2), (e) at least one constraint result exists, (f) no error-level diagnostics (only import warning allowed).

Fix any integration issues discovered in step-15. Ensure the parse-compile-eval pipeline handles all three features together correctly. Verify all existing tests still pass with cargo test --workspace. Common issues: tree-sitter grammar parsing 'sub' correctly (verified: grammar.js has sub_declaration rule), compiler sub_component compilation working (verified: lib.rs:782-797), or scoped ID format mismatch. If all passes, this is a no-op.

design_decisions.decision: Use 'ParentName.sub_name' as the entity in scoped ValueCellIds for sub-component members
design_decisions.rationale: Enables dot-notation access (e.g., Parent.rib.height) via string-based scoping without changing the ValueCellId struct. The entity field becomes a dotted path like 'Parent.rib' with the member being the child param name. This is consistent with the language spec's member access syntax and works with the existing ValueCellId::new(entity, member) API. ValueCellId already accepts arbitrary strings for entity (identity.rs:60-67).

design_decisions.decision: Sub-component elaboration happens in Engine::eval() rather than in the compiler
design_decisions.rationale: The compiler operates per-structure (compile_structure) and doesn't have access to the full module context needed to resolve structure_name references across templates. The Engine processes all templates and can look up referenced structures by name. This keeps the compiler's role as a per-structure compilation pass, with cross-structure resolution happening at the evaluation layer.

design_decisions.decision: Use a child-local ValueMap for child expression evaluation, then merge results into the global map with scoped IDs
design_decisions.rationale: Child template expressions reference ValueCellIds with the child's original entity name (e.g. ('Child','height')). When elaborating under a parent, we need a temporary ValueMap where these original IDs resolve to the arg-provided or default values. After evaluation, results are inserted into the global ValueMap with scoped IDs ('Parent.rib','height'). This avoids mutating compiled expressions while correctly evaluating child bindings.

design_decisions.decision: EvaluationGraph also gets sub-component nodes (not just Engine::eval())
design_decisions.rationale: The graph is used for incremental evaluation (dirty cone computation, demand tracking, reverse dependency index). Without sub-component nodes in the graph, incremental edit_param() wouldn't know about parent-child dependencies. Even though full incremental sub-component support may not be exercised yet, having the nodes ensures correct graph topology from the start.

design_decisions.decision: Import handling and stdlib function evaluation are verification-only steps (no new implementation expected)
design_decisions.rationale: Both features are fully implemented and tested at the compiler and expression-evaluation layers. Adding Engine-level verification tests confirms the end-to-end path works through the Engine facade and provides regression coverage for the full pipeline.

design_decisions.decision: Sub-component resolution is intra-module only (no cross-module lookup)
design_decisions.rationale: Cross-module sub-structure references require the module loader (M5). For current scope, sub-components can only reference templates within the same CompiledModule. If structure_name is not found in module.templates, a diagnostic is emitted and the sub-component is skipped. This aligns with the milestone progression.

design_decisions.decision: Single-level sub-component elaboration (no recursive nesting in this task)
design_decisions.rationale: Recursive sub-component nesting (sub within a sub) adds complexity (cycle detection, deeply scoped IDs like 'A.b.c'). For the architecture-critical baseline, one level proves the pattern works. Recursive nesting can be added in M5 'Language Breadth'.

design_decisions.decision: Place sub-component elaboration after the two-pass parent evaluation but before the resolution phase in Engine::eval()
design_decisions.rationale: Sub-component arg expressions reference parent param values, which are populated in pass 1 (params, lines 600-677). Sub-component let-bindings need fully evaluated child param values. The resolution phase (constraint solver for auto params, starting at line 744) should see the complete value map including sub-component values. This ordering ensures all dependencies are satisfied.

design_decisions.decision: Graph nodes for sub-components keep child-original expressions; remapping happens at eval time only
design_decisions.rationale: Rewriting ValueRef IDs in expressions would create a divergence between the graph's expressions and the compiled template's expressions, complicating debugging and snapshot comparison. Instead, EvaluationGraph stores sub-component nodes with scoped IDs but the default_expr still references child-original IDs. The Engine uses the child-local ValueMap pattern to evaluate correctly. The graph nodes exist primarily for topology (dependency tracking, demand) rather than direct expression evaluation.

reuse.how: Already exists and creates SubComponentDecl entries with name, structure_name, args, and content_hash. Use for building test fixtures with parent/child templates.

Reuse for ordering child let-binding evaluation. Same pattern as the parent two-pass evaluation in Engine::eval() lines 683-696: collect let cells into HashMap<NodeId, &CompiledExpr>, compute traces, sort topologically, then evaluate in order.

Evaluate child param arg expressions (against parent ValueMap) and child let-binding expressions (against child-local ValueMap). Already handles FunctionCall to stdlib dispatch, Undef propagation, and all binary/unary operations.

Extend to also create scoped ValueCellNode entries for sub-component children. The existing per-cell node creation (hash computation, ValueCellNode construction, insert into graph.value_cells) is the template for the new scoped nodes.

Build test fixtures with multiple templates (parent + child) and sub-component declarations. CompiledModuleBuilder.template() accepts multiple templates. CompiledModuleBuilder.import() adds import entries. These provide all the building blocks for test modules.
ValueCellId already accepts arbitrary strings for entity. Use 'Parent.sub_name' as entity for scoped IDs without any type changes needed.

Replicate the pass-1 (param default eval with journal events + cache recording) and pass-2 (topologically sorted let eval with journal events + cache recording) patterns for child template evaluation within the sub-component elaboration loop. Same Instant-based timing, same EvalEvent recording, same CachedResult::Value construction.

Follow same test structure: build module via builders or fixtures, create Engine with MockConstraintChecker (from mocks.rs), call eval(), assert values with .get() and .as_f64(). Also demonstrates parse-compile-eval E2E pattern (e2e_parse_compile_eval_auto_param at line 135).
Creates Value::Scalar with LENGTH dimension from millimeter values (converts to SI internally). Used throughout test fixtures and assertions.
